metadata.name: SWIFT Customer Security Controls Framework
metadata.description: The SWIFT Customer Security Controls Framework (CSCF) establishes mandatory and advisory security controls for all SWIFT users to secure their local SWIFT-related infrastructure and protect the wider SWIFT network from cyber threats.
metadata.source_organization: SWIFT (Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication)
metadata.classification_reasoning: The SWIFT CSCF is a certification framework requiring annual attestation and independent assessment. SWIFT users must attest compliance annually, with mandatory controls that must be implemented.
metadata.framework_purpose: To establish mandatory security controls for SWIFT users to protect the global financial messaging network and reduce cyber fraud risks in financial transactions.
metadata.framework_scope: All SWIFT users including banks, financial institutions, corporates, and service providers connected to the SWIFT network.
framework_objectives: Secure the local SWIFT environment against cyber attacks
Protect credentials and prevent unauthorized access
Detect anomalous activity on systems and transactions
Respond to and contain cyber incidents effectively
Share threat intelligence with the SWIFT community
metadata.target_audience: CISOs, IT Security Teams, SWIFT Administrators, Compliance Officers, and Internal Auditors of SWIFT member institutions
metadata.certification_body: SWIFT (Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication)
metadata.penalty_for_non_compliance: Non-compliance may result in removal from the SWIFT network, reputational damage, and increased scrutiny from regulators and counterparties.
adoption_approach: Identify SWIFT architecture type and applicable controls
Conduct gap assessment against mandatory controls
Implement mandatory controls first, then advisory controls
Complete self-assessment using SWIFT's KYC-SA application
Submit attestation to SWIFT before deadline

controls.title: SWIFT Environment Protection
controls.description: Ensure the protection of the user's local SWIFT infrastructure from potentially compromised elements of the general IT environment and external environment.
controls.full_text: The user's SWIFT infrastructure must be protected from potentially compromised elements of the general IT environment and external environment. This control ensures segregation of the SWIFT environment from the general IT environment through network segmentation, firewalls, and access controls.
evidence_requirements: Network diagrams showing SWIFT zone segregation

controls.title: Operating System Privileged Account Control
controls.description: Restrict and control the allocation and usage of administrator-level operating system accounts.
controls.full_text: Administrator-level operating system accounts must be restricted to personnel with a genuine business need, with controlled and monitored usage. Privileged access should be limited, logged, and regularly reviewed.

controls.title: Virtualisation Platform Protection
controls.description: Secure virtualisation platforms hosting SWIFT-related components with the same security controls as physical infrastructure.
controls.full_text: Where virtualisation is used to host SWIFT-related components, the virtualisation platform must be secured to the same standard as physical SWIFT infrastructure. This includes protecting hypervisors, virtual switches, and management interfaces.

controls.title: Restriction of Internet Access
controls.description: Prevent direct internet access from operator PCs and systems within the SWIFT secure zone.
controls.full_text: Operator PCs used to access SWIFT applications and systems within the secure zone must not have direct internet access. If internet access is required for business purposes, it must be provided through a separate, physically or logically isolated system.

controls.title: Internal Data Flow Security
controls.description: Ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity of data flows within the local SWIFT infrastructure.
controls.full_text: Data flowing between components within the local SWIFT infrastructure must be protected to ensure confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity. This includes implementing encryption, integrity checks, and authentication between SWIFT components.
evidence_requirements: Data flow diagrams with encryption points

controls.title: Security Updates
controls.description: Minimise the occurrence of known technical vulnerabilities through timely security updates.
controls.full_text: Security updates must be applied in a timely manner to all SWIFT-related components including operating systems, database software, and application software. A patch management process must be established and followed.

controls.title: System Hardening
controls.description: Reduce the cyber attack surface of SWIFT-related components by hardening system configurations.
controls.full_text: All SWIFT-related systems must be hardened by disabling unnecessary services, removing unnecessary software, and configuring security settings according to vendor guidelines and industry best practices.

controls.title: Back-office Data Flow Security
controls.description: Ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity of data flows between SWIFT infrastructure and back-office systems.
controls.full_text: Data flowing between the SWIFT infrastructure and connected back-office applications must be protected. Connections should use encrypted channels and authenticated interfaces.

controls.title: External Transmission Data Protection
controls.description: Protect the confidentiality of SWIFT-related data transmitted over external networks.
controls.full_text: SWIFT-related data transmitted to external parties or service providers must be protected using encryption and secure transmission protocols. This applies to all external data exchanges.

controls.title: Operator Session Confidentiality and Integrity
controls.description: Protect the confidentiality and integrity of interactive operator sessions connecting to the SWIFT infrastructure.
controls.full_text: Operator sessions to SWIFT infrastructure must be protected using encrypted connections (e.g., RDP with NLA, SSH). Session data must be protected from eavesdropping and tampering.

controls.title: Vulnerability Scanning
controls.description: Identify known vulnerabilities within the local SWIFT environment through regular vulnerability scanning.
controls.full_text: Regular vulnerability scans must be performed on all SWIFT-related systems to identify known vulnerabilities. Identified vulnerabilities must be remediated based on risk assessment.

controls.title: Outsourced Critical Activity Protection
controls.description: Ensure that outsourced SWIFT-related activities are protected with appropriate security controls.
controls.full_text: Where SWIFT-related activities are outsourced, the outsourcing arrangements must include appropriate security controls equivalent to those required for in-house operations.

controls.title: Transaction Business Controls
controls.description: Implement business controls to detect and prevent fraudulent transactions.
controls.full_text: Business controls must be implemented to detect anomalous transaction activity and prevent fraudulent payments. This includes verification processes, payment limits, and dual authorization requirements.

controls.title: Application Hardening
controls.description: Reduce the attack surface of SWIFT-related applications through security hardening.
controls.full_text: SWIFT-related applications must be hardened by configuring security settings, disabling unnecessary features, and applying vendor security recommendations.

controls.title: RMA Business Controls
controls.description: Implement controls over Relationship Management Application (RMA) to prevent unauthorized correspondent relationships.
controls.full_text: Controls must be implemented to manage SWIFT Relationship Management Application (RMA) records, ensuring only authorized correspondent relationships are maintained and regularly reviewed.

controls.title: Physical Security
controls.description: Prevent unauthorized physical access to sensitive equipment, systems, and documentation.
controls.full_text: Physical access to SWIFT equipment, systems, and sensitive areas must be restricted to authorized personnel only. Physical security controls including access controls, surveillance, and environmental protection must be implemented.

controls.title: Password Policy
controls.description: Ensure password policies are implemented and enforced for all SWIFT-related accounts.
controls.full_text: Strong password policies must be implemented for all SWIFT-related accounts, including complexity requirements, regular password changes, and protection against password reuse.

controls.title: Multi-factor Authentication
controls.description: Implement multi-factor authentication for access to SWIFT-related applications and systems.
controls.full_text: Multi-factor authentication must be implemented for operator access to SWIFT-related applications and systems. At least two independent authentication factors must be required.

controls.title: Logging and Monitoring
controls.description: Record security events and detect abnormal behaviour through comprehensive logging and monitoring.
controls.full_text: Security events on all SWIFT-related systems must be logged and monitored. Logs must be protected from tampering and retained for a sufficient period to support incident investigation.

controls.title: Intrusion Detection
controls.description: Detect and alert on anomalous network activity and potential intrusions within the SWIFT environment.
controls.full_text: Intrusion detection capabilities must be implemented to detect potential cyber attacks and anomalous activity within the SWIFT environment. Alerts must be reviewed and responded to promptly.

controls.title: Cyber Incident Response Planning
controls.description: Define and test a cyber incident response plan for SWIFT-related security incidents.
controls.full_text: A cyber incident response plan specific to SWIFT-related incidents must be developed, documented, and regularly tested. The plan must include escalation procedures, communication protocols, and recovery steps.

controls.title: Security Training and Awareness
controls.description: Ensure all staff with access to SWIFT systems receive appropriate security training.
controls.full_text: Staff with access to SWIFT-related systems must receive appropriate security training and awareness education. Training must be provided on initial assignment and refreshed periodically.

controls.title: Penetration Testing
controls.description: Validate the operational effectiveness of security controls through penetration testing.
controls.full_text: Regular penetration testing must be performed on SWIFT-related infrastructure to validate the effectiveness of security controls. Testing should cover network, application, and social engineering vectors.

controls.title: Scenario-based Risk Assessment
controls.description: Perform regular risk assessments considering SWIFT-specific cyber threat scenarios.
controls.full_text: Risk assessments must be performed regularly considering SWIFT-specific cyber threat scenarios. Assessments should consider current threat intelligence and evolving attack techniques.

controls.title: Secure SWIFT Connectivity
controls.description: Ensure secure connectivity to the SWIFT network through approved connection methods.
controls.full_text: Connectivity to the SWIFT network must be established through SWIFT-approved methods (Alliance Lite2, Alliance Connect, Service Bureau). Connection security must be maintained and monitored.

controls.title: Token Management
controls.description: Securely manage SWIFT tokens and cryptographic keys used for authentication and signing.
controls.full_text: SWIFT tokens and cryptographic keys must be securely managed throughout their lifecycle, including secure storage, controlled distribution, and proper destruction.

controls.title: Operator Credential Protection
controls.description: Protect operator credentials from theft, misuse, and unauthorized disclosure.
controls.full_text: Operator credentials used to access SWIFT systems must be protected from theft and misuse. This includes secure credential storage, protection against credential harvesting, and monitoring for credential abuse.

controls.title: Database Integrity
controls.description: Ensure the integrity of SWIFT-related databases against unauthorized modification.
controls.full_text: SWIFT-related databases must be protected against unauthorized modification. Database integrity controls including access restrictions, audit logging, and integrity monitoring must be implemented.

controls.title: Malware Protection
controls.description: Protect SWIFT-related systems from malware infection.
controls.full_text: All SWIFT-related systems must be protected with anti-malware solutions. Malware definitions must be kept current and scans performed regularly.

controls.title: Software Integrity
controls.description: Verify the integrity of SWIFT software and prevent unauthorized modifications.
controls.full_text: The integrity of SWIFT software must be verified before installation and periodically thereafter. Controls must prevent unauthorized software modifications.

controls.title: Secure Backup and Recovery
controls.description: Maintain secure backups of SWIFT-related systems and data for recovery purposes.
controls.full_text: Backups of SWIFT-related systems and data must be maintained securely and tested regularly for recoverability. Backups must be protected from unauthorized access and tampering.
